phase-plan.purpose: Build the first complete in-house CRM layer for Grime Time by mirroring the useful structure of HubSpot inside Payload and Next.js: linked business records, search and queue views, compact operator UI, Stripe billing sync, and Payload-jobs-driven Resend sequences.
phase-plan.scope: In scope: first-party CRM schema, compact `/ops` CRM workspace, owner assignment, pipeline and stale-follow-up views, multi-role employee duties, strict customer/admin data boundaries, fastest reminder delivery to workers' normal inboxes, Stripe webhook reconciliation into billing records, Payload jobs and Resend sequences, demo data, and test coverage. Out of scope: reintroducing HubSpot or EngageBay, rebuilding every HubSpot setting screen, or exposing admin-only CRM controls in the customer portal.

file.description: Capability map from HubSpot primitives into first-party Payload CRM, Stripe sync, and sequence automation.
Product direction and design rules for the in-house CRM, billing, and lifecycle automation.
Billing operating model for Stripe-hosted invoices, portal-first self-service, subscriptions, refunds/credits, and onsite/out-of-band payment handling.
System-of-record boundaries for Payload, Stripe, Resend, Supabase, and jobs.
Daily operating model for role duties, queue triage rhythm, SLA expectations, and reminder delivery policy.
Frontend and code-quality standards for icon usage, file-size guardrails, literal centralization, and reusable extraction patterns.
Payload collections, globals, jobs, email adapter, and MCP/plugin boundaries.
Current CRM boundary where third-party sync is disabled and first-party CRM should take over.
Shared CRM option sets for status, priority, sources, tasks, and automation enrollment state.
Admin CRM workspace and compact operating console target.
Compact non-overlapping command-center shell for ops sections, including the CRM workspace tab.
First-party CRM tab surface using queue groups, metric chips, and inline detail loading.
Admin-only queue/summary feed for the compact CRM workspace.
Admin-only record detail feed for CRM list/detail interactions.
Admin-only CRM activity mutation route for inline note logging from the workspace.
Shared server-side query and detail helpers backing `/ops` and internal CRM APIs.
CRM activity-note creation helpers that map selected records into first-party timeline entries.
Portal navigation rules separating admin CRM tools from customer account tasks.
Portal shell composition (sidebar/inset/toolbar layering) that must avoid overlap and preserve conventional app chrome behavior.
Structured CRM detail rail with summary/related tabs and compact record context.
Inline internal note composer for the CRM detail rail.
Existing quote records that should feed internal opportunities and follow-up state.
First-party account records for households and commercial relationships.
First-party contact records for people, consent state, and preferred communication method.
Inbound lead records before conversion.
Quote and pipeline opportunity records.
Activity timeline records for notes, calls, and system events.
Sequence-definition builder records for in-app follow-up automation design.
Task records for follow-up, billing, and schedule work.
Sequence enrollment records for Payload jobs and Resend automation.
Internal invoice records that must reconcile with Stripe webhook events.
Recurring-plan records that will carry subscription state and billing cadence.
Service schedule records that connect CRM ownership, customer portal scheduling, and ops calendar views.
Environment contract for Stripe, Resend, cron, and optional Payload MCP setup.

task.description: Create the first-party CRM collections, relationships, stages, assignment rules, commercial-account fields, and shared field vocabulary needed for leads through retained customers. Initial collection scaffold plus first-pass form/quote lifecycle writes are now in repo; company-account user membership and account-linked customer documents are now wired into `users`, quotes, invoices, service plans, and service appointments, while the next pass deepens conversion rules, queue helpers, sequence definitions, and derived lifecycle behavior.

Build internal CRM API routes and query helpers for queue views, stale follow-up, task management, notes, activities, and detail summaries. Queue/detail GET routes plus search, task updates, opportunity stage actions, and note creation now exist; broader mutations and saved views remain.

Turn `/ops` into the compact CRM workspace with queue tables, tabs, tab groups, button groups, icons, badges, info icons, tooltips, priorities, and a non-overlapping list/detail layout that avoids overflow-heavy interactions. The first compact workspace tab is now live with search, stale-only filtering, quick actions, a structured detail rail, inline note logging, richer company detail, and an impersonation-aware customer preview toolbar that stays visible across frontend and portal routes; broader operator actions and stronger filtering still remain.

Implement Stripe webhook ingestion and idempotent reconciliation so internal invoice and service-plan state is trustworthy. The target billing model is now explicit: Stripe-hosted invoice pages and portal-first customer self-service, monthly consolidated invoicing for variable commercial terms accounts, subscriptions for stable recurring amounts, and first-party support for onsite/out-of-band payment recording plus refund/credit adjustments.

Implement Payload-jobs-driven Resend sequences with the fastest practical path first: reminders and digests to each worker's preferred existing inbox, then optional mailbox-provider OAuth expansion after core queue reliability.

Populate demo data and cover the CRM, billing, and customer account flows with unit, integration, and Playwright tests. CRM workspace integration tests and Playwright admin/frontend coverage are now in repo; realistic seeded scenarios are still missing.

Define the role matrix and operating rhythm so staff know expected daily duties, queue order, SLA response windows, escalation ownership, and support/refund policy queue behavior. Employees can hold multiple roles, and queue responsibilities are additive.

Codify and enforce boundary policy: public users and logged-in customers only see public + self/account-scoped data; customer-company admins can manage users within their own company only; staff/admin views can access cross-account data and internal docs.
Keep local MCP usage repeatable for Codex/Cursor by documenting and validating endpoint/key/transport health checks for payload_local and image_gen_local.
Use conventional shadcn app-shell patterns for sidebar and content insets so the portal avoids overlay collisions, horizontal overflow, and hidden admin toolbar controls.

Promote CRM workspace, scorecard, milestones, assets, and today board into first-class `/ops/*` routes. Keep command center as a current-user execution hub with deep links and URL query params into those pages.

Enforce deterministic customer preview in staff portal: `Customer home` binds to a stable non-admin `test_user` context, sidebar labels show active context (`admin` vs preview user), and admin-only nav/docs/settings hide while previewing customer scope.

Apply and enforce coding conventions for shared UI/system quality: icon usage standards, reduced copy-heavy/faded UI patterns, large-file extraction, constants/typed unions for repeated literals, and reusable utilities for recurring patterns.

questions.question: Should a new inbound form always start as a `lead`, with promotion to `contact` plus `account` only after qualification, or should residential requests create both immediately?
What default owner-routing rules should assign new leads, tasks, and overdue billing follow-up before a more advanced round-robin system exists?
Which sequence steps should remain fully automatic, and which should create internal tasks instead of auto-sending customer email?
Which commercial-specific queue and billing fields must be visible by default in `/ops` so companies are not buried behind residential assumptions?
After task completion, opportunity-stage advancement, and note entry, which inline CRM actions should land next: owner reassignment, lead qualification, or customer outreach logging?
Which accounts should default to Stripe autopay versus send-invoice terms, and when should monthly consolidated invoicing be required instead of a subscription?
Which billing adjustments need first-class admin quick actions earliest: paid onsite, paid by check, discount, courtesy credit, refund, or write-off?

Day-one impersonation scope is the marketing site plus the customer portal routes for dashboard, estimates, invoices, schedule, and account management. Internal staff views such as `/ops` and admin docs redirect out when the effective user is non-admin.
Fastest rollout: reminders go to each worker's preferred existing email address (personal or company) via Resend delivery, without requiring Google/Microsoft mailbox OAuth for day one.
Employees may hold multiple internal roles simultaneously; queue permissions and duties are additive across assigned roles.

Anything that exposes cross-user or cross-company data, internal docs, or operational controls is admin/staff-only. Customer and public surfaces remain constrained to public + self/account-scoped data.
Confirm final SLA targets for support classes: general support, billing support, refund requests, and policy/privacy requests.
Define who can approve, deny, and finalize refund outcomes, and which steps require ops-admin versus billing-followup role ownership.
Confirm discount hierarchy and constraints: should user-level overrides always take precedence over account-level defaults, and do both allow percentage + flat amount variants?
Company-admin customers can manage users and company-admin membership within their company and can view company billing context including invoices, subscriptions, and payment methods.
CRM workspace and other dense ops sections should be split into dedicated `/ops/*` routes instead of being crowded inside one command-center surface.
Internal command center should focus on currently logged-in worker duties and link out to specialized ops pages via URLs and query params.
Admin impersonation toolbar should remain top-layer and visible; sidebar/content layout must not obscure it.
Staff `Customer home` should default to a stable non-admin `test_user` preview identity for repeatable customer-flow checks.
Seed contract should retain three admin staff users (`bg@`, `pb@`, `de@`) and one non-admin preview user (`test_user`) for deterministic testing.
Adopt soft file-size guardrails and extraction triggers to prevent growth of monolithic components/routes.
Repeated status/stage/role and UI label literals should move to shared constants/typed unions instead of ad hoc strings spread across files.
